selection_rationale: Chose the 3 tickers where our consensus accuracy is worst: MSTR 47.8% at 1d (45.6% at 3h), ETH 49.8% at 1d (48.4% at 3h), XAG 49.4% at 1d (53.5% at 3h). These are where our signal system has NO edge.

key_predictive_features: BTC price (primary driver, 0.58 correlation, high beta)
NAV premium/discount to BTC holdings (818K BTC at avg $75.5K)
ATM dilution capacity ($42B total authorized, pace of issuance)
BTC yield metric (9.6% YTD 2026, Saylor's key KPI)
Interest rate on convertible debt vs BTC carry
Equity market sentiment (F&G, VIX) - MSTR amplifies equity moves
BTC ETF flows (competing capital sink reduces MSTR premium)
fundamental_drivers: 818K BTC treasury = ~4% circulating supply. Value directly tied to BTC price.
Software business generates cash flow but is immaterial to valuation
Convertible debt structure creates leveraged BTC exposure
Share dilution from $42B ATM programs is ongoing risk to per-share NAV
Analyst range: $54-$705 for 2026 - extreme dispersion signals model uncertainty

cross_asset_correlations.BTC-USD: 0.58 correlation, high beta. BTC is THE input.
cross_asset_correlations.QQQ/NQ: High correlation as MSTR trades as tech/growth name
cross_asset_correlations.Interest_rates: Inverse - higher rates pressure convertible debt value

seasonal_patterns: No strong seasonal pattern - dominated by BTC cycle
Tends to outperform during BTC bull runs and underperform during bear markets
Quarterly dilution events (ATM sales) create predictable selling pressure
papers_and_models: BTC-correlated NAV model: Price = alpha * (BTC_holdings * BTC_price) + beta * equity_premium
MSTR premium/discount mean-reverts to ~1.2-1.8x NAV over 60-day window
TradingKey model: $176-228 range for 2026, Strong Buy consensus
recommended_new_signals: BTC price cross-asset signal for MSTR (weight BTC signals 3x for MSTR decisions)
NAV premium/discount tracker (BUY when discount >20%, SELL when premium >80%)
ATM dilution pace signal (bearish when weekly dilution exceeds historical norm)
BTC ETF flow direction as leading indicator for MSTR
deep_dives.implementation_notes: MSTR is fundamentally a leveraged BTC play. Our technical signals (RSI, MACD, etc.) are mostly noise for this ticker. We need to either (1) weight BTC signals directly into MSTR decisions or (2) create a BTC-NAV premium model. Quickest win: when computing MSTR consensus, also include BTC signal consensus with 2-3x weight.

key_predictive_features: BTC price direction (ETH follows BTC with lag, higher beta in rallies)
ETH/BTC ratio (structural decline - mean reversion signals when ratio compresses too far)
On-chain MVRV (currently 5.8% above zero, historically signals markup phase start)
Gas fees / network activity (proxy for DeFi demand)
Staking yield + ETH supply dynamics (deflationary post-merge)
DeFi TVL trends (ETH captures value from ecosystem activity)
Funding rates on perpetuals (currently our best signal at 3h: 74.2%)
fundamental_drivers: MVRV at 5.8% above zero - historically buy signal yielding avg 98% gain
Citi cut target to $3,175 from $4,304 citing weak on-chain user activity
ETH/BTC ratio in structural decline - L2 fragmentation diluting value capture
Pectra upgrade potential catalyst but not imminent
Correlation with top 10 coins: only 0.14 - ETH underperforming crypto broadly
ETH staking yield provides floor but not catalyst for price movement

cross_asset_correlations.BTC-USD: High, but ETH/BTC ratio declining. ETH underperforms BTC in current cycle.
cross_asset_correlations.QQQ: Moderate correlation with equity risk appetite
cross_asset_correlations.Gas_fees: Low correlation - network activity not driving price currently

seasonal_patterns: Historically follows BTC halving cycle with 2-6 month lag
Q2-Q3 tends to be strongest for ETH in post-halving years
Major upgrade events create predictable pre/post move patterns
papers_and_models: MVRV-based trading model: BUY when MVRV < -10%, SELL when > 30%
ETH/BTC ratio mean reversion: BUY when ratio drops below 0.03
Funding rate momentum: positive sustained funding = bullish (our 74.2% at 3h confirms)
CoinCodex AI model predicting Q2 breakout above $3K
recommended_new_signals: ETH MVRV signal (add to on-chain module, currently BTC-only)
ETH/BTC ratio mean reversion signal (BUY when extreme underperformance)
DeFi TVL momentum signal (rising TVL = bullish for ETH)
ETH staking rate change signal (rising staking = supply reduction = bullish)
deep_dives.implementation_notes: ETH accuracy is essentially coin-flip because our signal system treats it like any other asset. ETH-specific on-chain metrics (MVRV, staking, TVL) would add alpha. The BTC following effect means our BTC signals should partially inform ETH decisions. Quick win: add ETH MVRV to on-chain module (currently BTC-only) and create ETH/BTC ratio signal.

key_predictive_features: Gold price direction (XAG follows XAU with higher beta)
Gold/Silver ratio (currently near 62, historical mean 60-65, compressing from 90)
Real yields (TIPS) - inverse correlation with precious metals
DXY (USD index) - inverse correlation (our DXY signal exists but limited data)
COT speculative positioning (our COT signal exists, check accuracy)
Industrial demand proxies (copper, solar installations, EV production)
CME margin requirements (triggered 35% flash crash from ATH $121.88)
COMEX inventory levels (declining = physical shortage = bullish)
fundamental_drivers: 5th consecutive year of supply deficit (200M oz shortage in 2025)
Mine output +2% to 844M oz but record industrial demand (solar, AI, EVs)
CME margin hikes caused brutal 35% single-day plunge from $121.88 ATH
Recovering at $73, well below ATH. Goldman targets $85-100 avg, Citi targets $110 H2
Strait of Hormuz blockade driving inflation fears = higher for longer rates headwind
Central bank gold buying (25-30% of global production) supports gold-silver complex

cross_asset_correlations.XAU-USD: Very high correlation (0.85+). Gold leads, silver amplifies.
cross_asset_correlations.DXY: Inverse correlation. USD weakness = metals strength.
cross_asset_correlations.TIP/Real_yields: Inverse. Rising real yields hurt non-yielding metals.
cross_asset_correlations.Oil: Moderate positive currently (both benefiting from Iran crisis).

seasonal_patterns: Seasonal strength in Q1 and Q4 (electronics, solar manufacturing cycles)
Weakness typical in Q2-Q3 (low seasonal industrial demand)
BUT: current geopolitical premium overrides seasonal patterns
India wedding/festival season (Oct-Dec) drives physical demand
papers_and_models: Gold/Silver ratio mean reversion: when ratio > 80, go long silver
COT speculative net position contrarian: extreme longs = sell signal
Physical silver inventory depletion model (COMEX registered declining)
Real yield correlation model: silver = f(gold, DXY, TIPS, copper)
recommended_new_signals: COMEX registered inventory change signal (declining inventory = bullish)
Silver ETF flow signal (SLV inflows/outflows as sentiment proxy)
CME margin requirement change signal (margin hikes = near-term bearish)
Physical premium signal (LBMA premium above spot = bullish)
Improve existing COT signal with speculative positioning percentile
deep_dives.implementation_notes: XAG accuracy is coin-flip at 1d but slightly better at 3h (53.5%). The 3h edge comes from short-term momentum signals. For 1d, we need better fundamental drivers: supply deficit tracking, COT positioning, and real yield correlation. Quick win: improve the existing metals_cross_asset and COT signals with more granular data. Also: the CME margin hike risk is untracked - a new signal for margin requirement changes could prevent flash crash losses.
